summary: Large item-system resplit of the monolithic src/melee/it/it_2725.c into smaller translation units: it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako. The PR body says the split was driven by repeated floats/strings plus filename evidence such as __assert("itmaplib.c", ...), __assert("itanimlist.c", ...), struct it_MObjInfo it_803F1F90, and the "can t init zako pos\n" string. All new units were matching and linked except itmaplib, which was intentionally left NonMatching in configure.py. This improved item decomp organization and decomp.dev reported +4396 matched code bytes, +34912 linked code bytes, +17004 matched data bytes, and +17212 linked data bytes.

key_files.role: original large item TU, reduced to the residual it_2725 range
new item map-collision TU; left NonMatching
prototype and extern cleanup after moving functions to module headers
semantic struct renames and size assertion for item attrs/vars
Item xDD4_itemVar union updated to use semantic members
new text/data/sdata/sdata2 ranges for the resplit TUs
symbol scope/local-label updates for split-local literals and data
build object list updated; all new item TUs Matching except itmaplib
deleted old placeholder/data TU as data ownership moved into source splits

what_changed: Split src/melee/it/it_2725.c into eight item translation units with address ranges in splits.txt: it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako.
Added module headers it_279C.h, ithitbox.h, itmaplib.h, itmaterial.h, iteffect.h, itanimlist.h, and itzako.h, then removed many moved prototypes from it_2725.h.
Updated configure.py from one NonMatching it_2725 object to Matching it_2725 plus Matching ithitbox, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, itzako, and NonMatching itmaplib.
Deleted the old src/melee/it/it_3F14.c data/placeholder file and adjusted exported data ownership through the new TUs and headers.
Updated symbols.txt so many literals/jumptables became local @NNN labels or local-scope data after the split, and corrected some sbss object sizes for item globals.

Retargeted includes across item, fighter, and ground/stage files so callers include the narrower owner header, e.g. it_279C.h, ithitbox.h, itmaplib.h, itanimlist.h, iteffect.h, itmaterial.h, or itzako.h instead of only it_2725.h.

Renamed several address-based item structs to more semantic names: it_279D to itPokemonSpawn, it_27B5 to itZako, it_27CE to itGamewatch, it_27CF to itYoshiEggLay, it_2F28 to itUnk2, and it_2E6A_ItemVars_1 to itYaku.
Added STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(itPokemonSpawn_DatAttrs) == 0x2BC) and gave the Pokémon spawn weights array the explicit name pokemon_spawn_weights.
Made a small baselib jobj.h cleanup by removing commented-out assert/dirty-matrix expansion around HSD_JObjAddScaleX/Y/Z while leaving the active HSD_ASSERT and JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT logic.
smart_moves: Used concrete split evidence from repeated floats/strings and embedded filename clues instead of only guessing from function order; the PR body explicitly cites __assert("itmaplib.c", ...), __assert("itanimlist.c", ...), struct it_MObjInfo it_803F1F90, and "can t init zako pos\n".
Kept low-confidence names conservative: it_2725 remained address-like despite a suggested itcommon name, and it_279C stayed address-like despite a low-confidence itspecial suggestion.
Isolated the unsolved map-collision cluster as itmaplib and left only that TU NonMatching, allowing the other new item TUs to become Matching/linked.
Moved prototypes into per-domain headers so include dependencies document ownership and do not keep all users tied to the monolithic it_2725.h.
Localized split-owned literals and data in symbols.txt, reducing false global exposure and matching how separate TUs own their constants.
Improved type readability with semantic xDD4 itemVar union members while preserving unknown field names where the field-level meaning was still weak.
decomp_lessons: When a large matched TU blocks further progress, revisit the split: repeated sdata2 floats, strings, assert filenames, and data-object names can reveal real source-file boundaries.
A resplit can temporarily show many broken old-unit matches in progress reports because symbols moved out of the old TU; evaluate paired new matches in the destination TUs.
Do not overname uncertain files just because a tool suggests a semantic label; address-style names are safer until stronger evidence appears.
After moving functions, also move prototypes, externs, and includes to the new owner headers or the old catch-all header will hide stale dependencies.
Use symbols.txt local scope and @NNN labels for per-TU literals/jumptables after a split; otherwise global placeholder names can misrepresent ownership.
Keep the one hard cluster NonMatching if necessary, but make the rest of the split link and match so future work has a smaller target.
naming_conventions: New item helper filenames use short it-prefixed domain names: ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, and itzako.
Files with weak semantic evidence retained address-style names: it_2725 and it_279C.
Header guards introduced include GALE01_IT_2725, GALE01_IT_279C, GALE01_ITANIMLIST, GALE01_IT_EFFECT, GALE01_IT_HITBOX, GALE01_ITMAPLIB, GALE01_ITMATERIAL, and GALE01_ITZAKO.

Address-like item data structs were renamed to behavior/item-domain names: itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars, itPokemonSpawn_DatAttrs, itZako_ItemVars, itZako_DatAttrs, itGamewatch_ItemVars, itYoshiEggLay_ItemVars, itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs, itUnk2_ItemVars, itUnk2_DatAttrs, and itYaku_ItemVars.
Item union members were updated to lowercase semantic fields such as pokemon_spawn, zako, gamewatch, yoshiegglay, unk2, and yaku.
Anonymous or still-unidentified functions kept existing address-style names such as it_8027xxxx and fn_80277D8C rather than receiving speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: splits.txt carved it_2725's original text range into contiguous TUs: it_2725 0x80272560-0x802753DC, ithitbox 0x802753DC-0x80275BC8, itmaplib 0x80275BC8-0x80277D08, itmaterial 0x80277D08-0x8027870C, iteffect 0x8027870C-0x80278F2C, itanimlist 0x80278F2C-0x80279C48, it_279C 0x80279C48-0x8027B5B0, and itzako 0x8027B5B0-0x8027CF00.
configure.py encoded the practical matching state: itmaplib was the only new TU left as NonMatching.
sdata2_ordering helpers were added in moved code, including itanimlist and itzako, to preserve literal ordering for matching.
PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK and matching-sensitive inline helpers moved with their functions instead of being simplified away.
Assert strings in code served as source-filename anchors: __assert("itmaplib.c", 0x7F, "0") and __assert("itanimlist.c", 0x8B, "0").
decomp.dev reported 157 new matches and progress in unmatched itmaplib functions, while many listed broken matches were from the old it_2725 unit after relocation.
review_feedback: ribbanya approved the PR with: "This is really useful, thank you."
No inline review comments or requested changes were present in the dump.

decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 progress: matched code 68.60% (+0.11%, +4396 bytes), linked code 41.36% (+0.90%, +34912 bytes), matched data 41.17% (+1.40%, +17004 bytes), and linked data 34.93% (+1.42%, +17212 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that make src/melee/it/itmaplib.c Matching.
Search later history for semantic renames of it_2725 or it_279C.
Search for follow-up refinements to itPokemonSpawn_DatAttrs and pokemon_spawn_weights.
Find PRs reducing the post-2546 remaining item TUs/functions count from 20/84.
Search for additional source-filename clues using __assert("itmaplib.c") or __assert("itanimlist.c").
